name: PERICARDIAL MESOTHELIOMA
definition: PRIMARY MALIGNANT PERICARDIAL MESOTHELIOMA malignant primary neoplasm arising from mesothelial cells of the pericardium

demographics.Prevalence: 0.0022% at autopsy; < 1% of all mesotheliomas; 50% of all primary pericardial
demographics.Path: multiple coalescing pericardial masses with obliteration of pericardial space;
demographics.Histo: epithelial > biphasic (mixed) > fibrous (spindle cell); biphasic tumor composed of

discriminating_features: irregular diffuse pericardial thickening / cardiac encasement by soft-tissue masses
± invasion of adjacent vascular + anatomic structures

CXR: irregular diffuse pericardial thickening / cardiac encasement by soft-tissue masses
± invasion of adjacent vascular + anatomic structures
heterogeneous enhancement with involvement of parietal + visceral layers of pericardium

clinical.Prognosis: 6 weeks - 15 months survival, regardless of therapy
